title: populus × canadensis f. aurea
intro: Populus × canadensis f. aurea, also known as golden poplar or western poplar, is a cultivar of the hybrid black cottonwood (Populus trichocarpa x Populus deltoides) tree, known for its striking golden-yellow leaves. The bark and leaves of the golden poplar have been used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it beneficial for treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Populus × canadensis f. aurea include its ability to reduce inflammation, ease coughs, and support the immune system. Golden poplar is often prepared in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of conditions, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat colds and flu.
